npc_attitude: Recognizes him by posture and gear before he is close enough to speak. Waits without hand on weapon.
concrete_content: Patrol on the Weather Hills is short two men — pulled north without explanation ten days ago. He has been running the circuit alone. Asks if the north reported anything unusual.
tension: He shares the gap in coverage as a peer, not a report. Expects Aranath to know what it means.
traveller_stance: Gives the countersign. Receives the patrol intelligence. Files the missing men.

npc_attitude: More guarded than the greeting would usually warrant. Something has made him careful with his own.
concrete_content: A supply cache on the red hills road — three nicks on the eastern stone — was emptied three weeks ago and not replenished. Someone used it and did not report it. The cache-keeper in Bree does not know who.
tension: A breach in their network, reported to the only other Ranger he has trusted with it. The breach could mean desertion, capture, or something else entirely.
traveller_stance: Does not fill the silence after. Lets the weight of the missing report sit between them.

npc_attitude: Tense in a way that has become posture. Has been reading a compromised landscape for too long alone.
concrete_content: Three farms on the Minhiriath road gone dark in sequence — doors open, fires cold, animals still penned. Moving south to south-east. Not raiders — something methodical.
tension: He wants Aranath on the northern circuit while he continues south. He cannot cover both and he does not trust anyone else he has met on this road in a month.
traveller_stance: Runs the calculation of what the northern circuit costs in time. Then agrees.

npc_attitude: Formal. The posture of a man who was told to show respect to Elves and has remembered it.
concrete_content: He asks whether she has seen movement further north. Three abandoned steadings he does not remember being empty before. He has no other Elf-source on this circuit.
tension: He is doing his duty. She is a resource he would not normally have. There is no warmth in it but the respect is real.
traveller_stance: Answers what she knows. Sees in him the grandson of the man she knew. Does not say so.

npc_attitude: The caution is wearing thin. He has been alone long enough that the formal approach has cracked.
concrete_content: He asks directly: did she know Cardolan, before. Not as a test — as a question from a man whose entire history is book-knowledge, standing in the place where it happened.
tension: He wants to know what it was actually like. She is the only person he has ever met who could tell him. He is slightly afraid she will refuse.
traveller_stance: Considers whether to answer fully. Gives him one true thing and watches what he does with it.

npc_attitude: He watched her on the road for ten minutes before speaking. Something in the way she moves through a landscape of ruins stopped him from the usual approach.
concrete_content: He asks, carefully, if she is well. The question lands differently than it should. He does not know why he asked it.
tension: The question is more perceptive than expected from a mortal on a cold road. She is not well. She is not going to say so.
traveller_stance: Recognizes real attention. Gives him the minimum that is also true.

npc_attitude: They move through the dusk as if they belong to it. One pauses and looks at him for a long moment, then continues.
concrete_content: They are heading west — always west, in the end. The one who paused has Rivendell-silver in his gear, a design he has seen in the archives at Fornost. He has read about this company. He did not expect to see them.
tension: They will not stop unless he speaks. The pause was acknowledgment, not invitation.
traveller_stance: Watches them pass without speaking. Is not certain he could have.

npc_attitude: One of them has already turned to face the road where he is approaching, waiting.
concrete_content: A patrol in the Trollshaws has gone silent — an Elven patrol, not a Ranger one. They noticed because the patrol was supposed to pass through the Elven road three days ago and did not. They are telling him because it is his circuit.
tension: They give him the information because he is Dúnedain and it concerns his land. Not a gift — what is done between those who watch the same darkness.
traveller_stance: Receives the news. Thanks them in Sindarin. Watches their faces when he does.

npc_attitude: The company stops when they see her. Not in surprise — in recognition. Someone makes a sound that is not quite a word.
concrete_content: One of them knows her name, not from meeting her but from a song about something she did in the First Age that she does not think of as notable. Hearing it described back to her is disorienting.
tension: They want to know where she is going. They mean it as kin-concern, not challenge.
traveller_stance: Tells them the direction but not the reason. There is kindness in not explaining.

npc_attitude: The eldest of the group speaks, the others are quiet. Careful with her.
concrete_content: He mentions someone who sailed — someone she knew. He says the name gently, watching her face, offering it as if asking whether she already knew. She did not.
tension: He is not being cruel. He thought she should know. She is not yet certain she is grateful.
traveller_stance: Does not react immediately. Then asks: when. Listens to the answer.

npc_attitude: One breaks away from the group and comes to her directly. The others continue without stopping.
concrete_content: He says quietly that this company is the last to use this road for some time. He does not explain why. He looks at her the way you look at someone choosing to stand in a place others are choosing to leave.
tension: He is not asking her to reconsider. He is witnessing her choice. The acknowledgment is both comfort and weight.
traveller_stance: Holds his gaze. Then: I know.

npc_attitude: One steps into view deliberately and stops. Not a threat — an acknowledgment. He is letting him see.
concrete_content: He leaves a carved marker at the trail junction — a Wose sign meaning this path is watched, use it. The marker was not there this morning.
tension: This is the Woses giving what they give: knowledge of the land, in their own language, to those who know how to read it.
traveller_stance: Reads the sign correctly. Leaves dried meat at the base of the marker stone. Walks on.

npc_attitude: Two of them moving parallel to the road, matching his pace exactly. Escorting without being asked.
concrete_content: They stop when the road bends and a disturbed barrow-mound comes into view ahead. They will not go closer. The escort ends here and both of them know it.
tension: They have told him what they can tell him. He continues from this point alone.
traveller_stance: Notes where they stopped. Approaches the barrow with full attention.

npc_attitude: A low deliberate sound from the scrub — not voice, not animal. A warning.
concrete_content: Something on the trail ahead that the Woses do not want him to walk into. They will not show themselves to explain what it is.
tension: The warning was given. The explanation was not. He has enough.
traveller_stance: Stops. Waits. Takes the longer path around.

npc_attitude: One is standing at the road's edge, so still she almost passed him. He is old — older than he looks.
concrete_content: He carries a staff carved with marks she has seen before. Not Wose marks. Older ones. Marks that were used in the late First Age for a specific purpose that almost no one living remembers.
tension: He knows what those marks mean. She knows what those marks mean. They are possibly the only two on this road who do.
traveller_stance: Stops. Looks at the staff without pointing at it. Then meets his eyes.

npc_attitude: A group from the treeline, still and watching. Not decided yet whether to acknowledge her.
concrete_content: One of the oldest wears a bone-carved token at his neck — made during the Wars of the First Age, for a specific purpose. The token should not exist anymore. She knew who made them.
tension: She has not seen one of those tokens in ages beyond counting. Its presence here, in T.A. 1950, worn by a Wose in Minhiriath, is something that requires more thought than she can give it right now.
traveller_stance: Does not react to the token. Gives them the old sign. Waits.

npc_attitude: One comes to the edge of the road and looks at her directly. The others stay back.
concrete_content: He points — once, briefly — at the road ahead. Then at the sky to the north. Then he draws a specific shape in the air with one finger that she has not seen drawn by a living hand in two thousand years.
tension: Someone taught a Wose a warning-sign from the First Age. That is not possible through memory alone. Something showed it to him recently.
traveller_stance: Returns the sign. Asks with her hands: how recently. He holds up three fingers.

npc_attitude: Names what he has, names what he needs. Expects the same. Will not haggle but will deal.
concrete_content: He has current maps of the mine-tracks in the Misty Mountain foothills — not for sale, but tradeable for road conditions. He wants to know what the barrow-field road looks like right now.
tension: Exact, bounded, symmetrical. He will not give more than he gets and will not try to take more than offered.
traveller_stance: Gives him the road conditions precisely. Watches him update a small book.

npc_attitude: Testing what the traveller knows before committing to anything.
concrete_content: He found something in a collapsed shaft — old, made, pre-Númenórean. He will not describe it precisely until he knows who he is talking to. He asks first about the shaft's location to see if Aranath knows it.
tension: He is deciding whether this is someone who can help identify old artifacts or someone who will try to take them.
traveller_stance: Describes the shaft's location without mentioning the artifact. Watches the Dwarf decide whether to trust further.

npc_attitude: Blunt. Not rude — constitutionally incapable of the social padding mortals use. Says what he means.
concrete_content: He is looking for a specific vein on a map drawn before Moria fell. He asks, without embarrassment, whether she was alive when Moria fell and whether she knows what the landmarks on the map were called before they got their current names.
tension: He does not know this is a strange question to ask. To him it is simply efficient.
traveller_stance: Was alive when Moria fell. Tells him the name the first landmark had before the map was drawn.

npc_attitude: Quieter than a Dwarf alone on the road usually is. Adjusting back to speech after a long time without it.
concrete_content: He says, after a pause, that he came out of the western passages two weeks ago. Fast. He does not elaborate but his hands have not stopped moving since he sat down.
tension: He is not going to say what was in the western passages. He is telling her he was there so she knows someone has been there recently.
traveller_stance: Does not ask what was in the passages. Asks where the western passages exit on the surface.

npc_attitude: Watching her the way someone watches a thing that has been around long enough to carry weight they cannot see.
concrete_content: He asks point-blank if she knew Durin. Not which Durin — just Durin. He wants to know how old she actually is. He asks because something in the western passages bore a mark only someone who was there at the beginning would recognize.
tension: He may be right that the mark requires a witness. She is not that witness — but she knows someone who was.
traveller_stance: Says she was not there at the beginning. Tells him she knows who was. Watches what he does with that.

npc_attitude: Gone into the bracken so fast he almost missed them. One small face still watching from cover.
concrete_content: The face belongs to a Harfoot who has decided Rangers are safe but not comfortable. The look is someone waiting until he is well past before coming back out.
tension: He is big and armed on their road. That is enough. He does not need to be a threat to be a problem.
traveller_stance: Does not slow. Does not make eye contact with the face in the bracken. Passes at normal pace.

npc_attitude: Working at the bog's edge. Appear to be ignoring him. Are not.
concrete_content: Two Stoors sorting a marsh catch. They have been camped here for weeks and know this bog the way a farmer knows a field. They will answer a direct question. They will not volunteer anything.
tension: They know about the ford. Whether they will say depends entirely on how he asks.
traveller_stance: Asks about the ford. Gets an answer, with conditions attached.

npc_attitude: On the road alone with a pack that is too large for him. He does not flinch when Aranath comes around the bend.
concrete_content: He is looking for a cousin who went east to trade and did not come back. He has been looking for three weeks. He asks if Aranath has seen a hobbit-sized camp east of the Weather Hills.
tension: He is more determined than afraid and it is the determination that is concerning. He is going to keep looking.
traveller_stance: Has not seen the camp. Asks for a description. Decides to watch for it.

npc_attitude: Vanished into the grass before she fully registered they were there. One left a half-eaten apple on the road.
concrete_content: The apple is still warm. They were eating lunch and she came around a bend and they simply ceased to be visible. The apple is real and sitting in the middle of the road.
tension: She has lived through ages beyond counting and a hobbit's abandoned lunch is briefly, absurdly touching.
traveller_stance: Picks up the apple. Sets it on a stone off the road so it is findable. Continues.

npc_attitude: Three of them at a campsite — cooking, arguing gently, not particularly interested in her arrival.
concrete_content: The argument is about whether the mushrooms in this region are worth the detour. One is passionately in favor. The others are unconvinced. They have strong opinions about mushrooms.
tension: They live so entirely in the immediate that the weight she carries does not register on them. She is briefly just a person near a fire.
traveller_stance: Sits down at the edge of the fire uninvited. Nobody objects.

npc_attitude: One sitting on a tussock above the waterline, watching the bog. Has been there long enough that his clothes are damp.
concrete_content: He says, without looking at her: the lights came back last night, closer than before. He says it the way someone reports weather — a fact, not a fear.
tension: He has been living next to the bog long enough to develop a working relationship with its dangers. He is not afraid of the lights. He is cautious, which is a different thing.
traveller_stance: Asks how close. He tells her. Recalibrates where she will camp.

npc_attitude: Blocking the road. Not drawn yet. Asking questions in the manner of men who expect to be answered.
concrete_content: Three of them. The captain wants to know what he is carrying and where he got it. There is a description circulating — partial, inaccurate enough that it probably does not match — of a Ranger carrying dispatches north of the Lefnui.
tension: He is being checked against a description. If the dispatches are found the conversation ends differently.
traveller_stance: Answers the question about cargo. Does not answer the question about origin. Keeps his hands visible.

npc_attitude: Four of them in a formation that closes the road when he stops. They positioned well before he arrived.
concrete_content: The captain has a specific bounty — not a vague report, an actual sum — for a Ranger carrying Arthedain dispatches. He is not certain this is the man. He is certain enough to try.
tension: The formation is the argument. What Aranath says in the next thirty seconds determines whether this stays a conversation.
traveller_stance: Has already counted them and found the formation wanting. Answers the first question with a question of his own.

npc_attitude: Formal obstruction. Following a pattern they were told to follow. The captain is professional about it.
concrete_content: There is a report from Umbar — circulating among men who work for southern interests — of a lone elf-blooded traveller moving north of the Lefnui. The description is vague. It fits her and does not fit her.
tension: He does not know what he is looking for precisely. She is clearly old enough to be what the report describes. He is deciding if challenging her is worth it.
traveller_stance: Lets him decide. Does not help him decide.

npc_attitude: Three of them. The captain's eyes do not move from her face. He knows something more specific.
concrete_content: There is a real bounty — recent, out of Umbar, substantial — for an elf woman travelling alone in this region. He knows what she is. He is calculating whether he can take her and what it will cost him.
tension: She is not afraid of three Corsairs. He cannot know this yet. What he can see is that she has not moved and has not looked away from him.
traveller_stance: Has already counted them and identified what each of them will do. Waits for his decision.

npc_attitude: Four of them, already in formation. One has moved behind her without her appearing to notice. She noticed.
concrete_content: She watched Númenor drown. She was at the shore when the wave came. These men are the inheritors of a specific corruption she has seen ruin entire bloodlines across the ages. The contempt she feels is not anger — it is something older and quieter.
tension: She has decided what she will do before they finish arranging themselves. The decision is not about fear.
traveller_stance: Has already decided. Lets them begin.

npc_attitude: Looks him over — armed, northern, alone — and decides he is not a threat before speaking.
concrete_content: Southern ford at the estuary washed out last month. The Gwathuirim crossing upstream is still passable but needs a guide. She knows a Stoor who will show it for information from the north, not coin.
tension: She has something he needs and is willing to deal. The deal is information for information.
traveller_stance: Names what he knows from the north. Watches her decide if it is enough.

npc_attitude: Assessing him. Her experience with Rangers on this road is mixed.
concrete_content: Saralainn from the eastern clan burned three farmsteads last month — not her clan. She says this with the precision of someone who expects to be blamed for it anyway. She needs word carried north to the garrison naming the eastern clan, not hers.
tension: She is asking him to carry a message that matters to her survival. She knows he might refuse. She asks anyway.
traveller_stance: Agrees to carry it. Specifies what north means.

npc_attitude: Stopped him on the road on impulse. Afraid and trying not to show it.
concrete_content: The lights in the marsh came back last night, closer than before. Three men went in after them last week. She does not know what happened to the three men but she knows nobody is going near the eastern bog.
tension: She needed to tell someone. The fear has been sitting in her chest for a week.
traveller_stance: Asks her which direction the lights move. Gets the answer. Adjusts the route.

npc_attitude: Curious about the traveller. Cautious, but the curiosity is winning.
concrete_content: The sandbanks at the estuary mouth shifted after last month's storm — the ford she is heading for does not exist anymore. The Gwathuirim know the new safe crossing but what they want for it is not coin.
tension: She is giving useful information without being asked. She wants company on the road to the next settlement, just until the exposed stretch is past.
traveller_stance: Offers the company. Walks with her to the settlement. Does not speak much.

npc_attitude: Cautious. Gives less than she knows. Reads the traveller before speaking.
concrete_content: Southern ford is gone. Saralainn burned three farmsteads west of the marsh last month — not her clan, another one, and she says it like the distinction matters. The coastal road has been empty since.
tension: She is giving the bad news and holding the actionable news back. The actionable news costs something.
traveller_stance: Notices what is not said. Asks for it.

npc_attitude: Suspicious. Gives nothing first. Watches the traveller's hands throughout.
concrete_content: The marsh is being watched — not by Saralainn. Three men went in last week following lights that moved. None came back. The Gwathuirim will not say what the lights are.
tension: She is afraid and trying not to show it. Saying it out loud makes it more real.
traveller_stance: Lets the silence last a beat. Then: I know what those lights are. I will not tell you it is safe to go near them.

npc_attitude: Stepped out of a side-track directly in front of him. Startled but not hostile.
concrete_content: Something has been taking animals from the high pasture — there and gone by midday, no tracks. He wants someone with him when he goes to look. He already asked his neighbors. They refused.
tension: He is not ashamed of not wanting to go alone. He is being practical.
traveller_stance: Asks what the schedule is. Goes with him.

npc_attitude: Watchful. Takes the time to look at the gear before speaking.
concrete_content: He has a Gondorian road-permit issued to his father, no longer valid. He does not know what to do with it. Asks if Aranath knows where the nearest garrison would reissue one.
tension: A practical question about a bureaucratic system he was never taught. Slightly embarrassing to ask.
traveller_stance: Gives him the garrison location and says the permit expired but they will reissue in person.

npc_attitude: Formal. Uncertain. Has not spoken to an Elf before and is trying to do it correctly.
concrete_content: He has been trying to read the old stones at the eastern ridge for two years — not the Gondorian stones, the older ones underneath. He asks if she can read them.
tension: He is right that she can. He is asking because he has exhausted every other option.
traveller_stance: Asks which stones. He describes them. She knows exactly what they are. Tells him what the inscription says.

npc_attitude: Came down from the ridge specifically to speak to her. Was watching the road from above.
concrete_content: Two people in his village have something wrong with them that started after they went near the old mine shaft. The healer does not know what it is. He is asking the only person on this road who looks old enough to have seen something like it before.
tension: He is out of options and he knows it. She looks like someone who has answers to questions people do not usually ask.
traveller_stance: Asks what the symptoms are. Listens. Asks about the mine shaft. Gives him what he needs without explaining how she knows it.

npc_attitude: Old man on the road, alone, no pack, no staff. Just standing.
concrete_content: He was a healer's apprentice at a Gondorian outpost abandoned after the Plague. He has been in this country ever since. He asks only whether she has seen the old mine at the eastern fork. He asks it with a calm that means he will go there alone if she says no.
tension: He is going regardless. The question is only whether she is curious enough about what is in that mine to go with him.
traveller_stance: Considers it. Does not decide immediately.

npc_attitude: Fresh territorial marks on a post at a track junction.
concrete_content: Clan claim on this stretch of road, placed within the last day. A patrol establishing presence after something happened nearby.
tension: He is on claimed ground. The claim will be enforced if he is seen on it.
traveller_stance: Reads the territorial mark. Adjusts the route to skirt the claim.

npc_attitude: Stopped him on the road. Not aggressive yet — testing first.
concrete_content: Three men. The speaker wants to know if he is Gondorian, Arthedain, or something else. The question has a correct answer and a wrong answer and Aranath needs to give the correct one.
tension: Dunlendings sort travellers by what they are, not who they are. The category matters here.
traveller_stance: Names himself Dúnedain, not Gondorian. Watches their faces.

npc_attitude: Letting him pass but just barely. One hand resting on a belt-knife.
concrete_content: He wants to know if Aranath has seen Gondorian soldiers on this road in the last week. Not asking for help — checking whether the road is being watched.
tension: The question is information-gathering. He answers it and the answer will determine whether Aranath's road gets easier or harder.
traveller_stance: Answers honestly. No soldiers seen. Watches the hand on the knife-belt relax slightly.

npc_attitude: Territorial marks on the road. Fresh, deliberate.
concrete_content: She has watched the rise and fall of the kingdom these marks oppose. The marks themselves are grief dressed as hostility — a people trying to hold what was taken with symbols on wood.
tension: She does not cross marked territory she has not been invited into. Not from fear — from respect for a kind of claim she understands better than they know.
traveller_stance: Takes the long way around.

npc_attitude: Two of them on the road. They step aside but not far. One watches her with the specific attention of someone looking for evidence of something he was told.
concrete_content: He was told Elves serve the enemy. He is looking at her for proof of this.
tension: She has been looked at this way before, in better centuries. The accusation is older than the people making it.
traveller_stance: Walks past slowly. Does not give him evidence. Does not correct him.

npc_attitude: A gathering visible from the ridge — twenty men at a flat stone, arranged in a circle.
concrete_content: The formation is pre-war. He has read about it in Ranger records. The last time this pattern was used was before the Battle of the Crossings of the Poros two centuries ago.
tension: They are binding themselves to something. When the Daen Iontis bind themselves, it becomes a military problem within months.
traveller_stance: Maps the location. Continues. This goes in the next report.

npc_attitude: Aftermath of a gathering — stone marked, fire still warm, men dispersing into the hills.
concrete_content: Three of them notice him on the road below and stop dispersing.
tension: He has two seconds to give them a reason not to make him the problem.
traveller_stance: Does not slow. Does not look at them directly. Moves with the ease of someone who belongs on this road.

npc_attitude: A ritual preparation at a flat stone. Seven men handling objects with deliberate care.
concrete_content: The objects are old. Older than Gondor, older than Arnor. She knows what they were originally used for. It is not what the Daen Iontis think they are used for.
tension: They have found something they do not understand and are building a belief system around it. The belief system will cause deaths.
traveller_stance: Passes without acknowledging. What they are doing is not her concern. What they will do because of it may be.

npc_attitude: A larger gathering on a ridge. The chant is audible from the road below.
concrete_content: The chant is a corruption — the words are Dunlending but the rhythm is from something spoken before the Dunlendings existed. She knows the rhythm. She knew the original context. Someone stripped the context away and handed them the form.
tension: Something taught these men a ritual from the First Age without giving them the meaning. That is not accidental.
traveller_stance: Does not stop. Does not show recognition. Files it.

npc_attitude: Moving through the treeline parallel to the road, tracking him without hiding it.
concrete_content: Three of them checking his route — they want to know where he is going because the path ahead splits and one branch is theirs. They will not approach but if he takes the wrong branch, that becomes a different conversation.
tension: He is being assessed from cover. The assessment determines what the road ahead costs him.
traveller_stance: Takes the western branch at the split. Watches them disappear from the eastern treeline.

npc_attitude: One of them standing on a rock above the trail, watching him below.
concrete_content: He has marked the trail — a stone moved to a specific position, a cut in bark. He is watching to see if the traveller reads the marks or misses them.
tension: This is a test. Reading the marks correctly may open something. Missing them closes it.
traveller_stance: Stops. Looks at the marked stone. Then looks up at the watcher. Moves the stone to the secondary position. Waits.

npc_attitude: Three of them working at the base of a specific oak. Re-marking something the storm displaced.
concrete_content: The marks they are cutting are older than the tree they cut them into. These marks were transferred here from something that no longer exists — she can see the formula, the deliberate preservation of a sign that should have died with whatever originally bore it.
tension: She knows what these marks mean. They have not been actively used since the Second Age.
traveller_stance: Does not approach. Does not startle them. When they look up and see her seeing the marks, she gives them the old sign.

npc_attitude: A group of them visible at the cape, still and watching. Not decided whether to acknowledge her.
concrete_content: One of the oldest has a specific carved token at his neck — she knew the hands that made that design. The token was made as a key to something, and whatever it opens has been waiting a long time.
tension: She has not seen that specific token in two thousand years. It should not be here. It is here.
traveller_stance: Gives them the acknowledgment sign. Waits.

npc_attitude: Three of them at a camp, debating. They stop when she approaches. One stands.
concrete_content: They are documenting disruptions in the Second Age road-wards on this stretch — places where the wards have been broken deliberately, from below. They are finding the disruptions moving north. They want to know if she has seen anything that maps to the same pattern.
tension: Their pattern and the things she has noticed point to the same source.
traveller_stance: Tells them what she has seen. Watches them understand what it means.

npc_attitude: One separates from the group and comes to her directly. His expression is not warm.
concrete_content: He says without preamble that he knows why she is still in Middle-earth and that he does not think it is entirely her choice. He thinks something is holding her here. He asks if she knows what it is.
tension: He may be right. She has never examined the possibility that staying is not fully chosen.
traveller_stance: Asks him what he means by holding. Listens to the answer.

npc_attitude: Three figures at a camp — clearly Elves, occupied with something that looks like both study and ritual.
concrete_content: He has read reports from Noldorin scholars who travel the old roads documenting Second Age wards. He did not expect to actually encounter them. They are doing work that intersects with his from a completely different angle.
tension: They are occupied. Interrupting would gain him nothing. But knowing they are on this road means something.
traveller_stance: Watches from respectful distance. Does not interrupt. Continues with the information filed.

npc_attitude: An Elf on the road, moving west. Moving with the weight of someone who has decided something final.
concrete_content: He pauses when he sees Aranath — a Dúnedain on this road is unusual. He studies him for a moment with a look that is not quite sympathy and not quite warning. He sees in Aranath the child of a people carrying what the Elves are leaving behind.
tension: The look acknowledges this without being able to do anything about it.
traveller_stance: Meets the look. Moves aside to let him pass.

npc_attitude: An Elf she does not know, moving west. The particular lightness of someone going home.
concrete_content: He sees her and stops. She is clearly not going west. The divergence is its own statement. He does not ask her to come — he knows she has been asked. He says only: it is still there.
tension: The it does not need to be named.
traveller_stance: Does not respond immediately. Then: I know.

npc_attitude: He was waiting on the road. He knew she would be here today. He does not explain how.
concrete_content: He has a sealed letter from someone at the Havens — someone she has not heard from in years. He will not say the message aloud. He hands her the letter and the seal is one she recognizes.
tension: She has not received a letter from the Havens in a very long time.
traveller_stance: Takes the letter. Does not open it while he watches.

npc_attitude: A farm family on the far side of a field. The child waves. The mother shades her eyes.
concrete_content: The farm is maintained — animals present, fire going. This road has been his circuit for six years. The child who waved is the grandchild of the child who waved the first time he came through.
tension: He has not mentioned this to anyone.
traveller_stance: Waves back. Continues.

npc_attitude: A farmer and a child on a far hill, working together. The child is trying to carry something too heavy.
concrete_content: The child will carry heavier things soon. The farmer will grow old. The child will carry those things alone eventually.
tension: She has watched this sequence more times than there are names for it. The scene is ordinary and inexorable at once.
traveller_stance: Watches them until they disappear over the hill. Then continues.

npc_attitude: A man mending a fence at the road's edge. Looks up. Speaks without preamble.
concrete_content: Strangers on the south road at night for a week — not camping, moving. No lights. He has not gone to look at the tracks because he has children.
tension: He is telling Aranath because Aranath looks like someone who deals with strangers-on-roads-at-night. He is right.
traveller_stance: Asks which direction they moved. Thanks him. Adjusts the patrol.

npc_attitude: A woman at a well, drawing water. Looks at the traveller. Then speaks.
concrete_content: Her husband went north three weeks ago with a trading party. The party came back without him. They said he stayed on voluntarily. She does not believe this.
tension: She is telling a stranger because she has told everyone she knows and no one will help. She is not asking for anything. She is saying it out loud one more time.
traveller_stance: Listens to the whole thing. Does not offer hope. Offers: I will remember his name.

npc_attitude: A merchant caravan moving north on the road. Three wagons, faster than the road warrants.
concrete_content: Tharbad-registered pennant — guild cloth. Moving fast means they have the same road intelligence he does, or something spooked them out of Tharbad in a hurry.
tension: Merchants moving fast on a bad road are a weather-vane.
traveller_stance: Notes the direction. Continues.

npc_attitude: A Tharbad factor at a wayhouse, drinking alone. Looks up when Aranath enters.
concrete_content: Stranded four days — the Gwathlo crossing closed without notice. Gondorian authority he thinks, though no Gondorian livery. He is losing money every day. He knows who closed the crossing, or believes he does, and will tell anyone who buys him a drink.
tension: The name costs him nothing to give and he wants to give it.
traveller_stance: Buys the drink. Gets the name.

npc_attitude: A merchant party on the far road, moving north. The city that made them is visible in how they carry themselves.
concrete_content: Tharbad was magnificent once. What it produces now are people who move with the posture of a place that remembers being great but has been spending that memory for centuries.
tension: She watched the city when it was great.
traveller_stance: Continues. Does not stop to watch.

npc_attitude: He saw them before they saw him. Three men in old ruins, too comfortable for vagrants.
concrete_content: A chokepoint at a bridge ahead — the only crossing for two miles. He has already identified the approach angles and the alternative.
tension: This is a tactical problem with a tactical solution. He does not need to fight them.
traveller_stance: Takes the ford upstream. Adds an hour. They never know he was there.

npc_attitude: Four men. He can see two swords and a bolt-case — crossbow somewhere he cannot yet locate.
concrete_content: They have not positioned well — visible from the ridge, obvious approach. The crossbow is the problem. The others he can manage. The crossbow requires a different decision.
tension: He is running the geometry of the encounter before it happens.
traveller_stance: Identifies where the crossbow must be given the bolt-case position. Plans accordingly.

npc_attitude: A camp in old ruins. Four men. She was watching them before they noticed the road.
concrete_content: They have been here at least a week — the refuse pile is old. They are waiting for something specific, not opportunistically raiding. Something on this road is worth waiting for.
tension: They are a symptom. She does not know yet of what.
traveller_stance: Notes the duration. Walks past on the far side of the ruin wall. They do not see her.

npc_attitude: She has seen men with this expression — boredom plus readiness — outside the walls of kingdoms she watched fall.
concrete_content: They have a captive. Someone sitting against the far wall, bound at the wrists. A traveller, by the gear still visible.
tension: She has seen this before, in better centuries and worse. The question of whether to intervene has different weight depending on who is asking it.
traveller_stance: Counts the men. Notes the captive's condition. Makes a decision.

npc_attitude: Fresh tracks crossing the road — heavy boots, irregular spacing, moving east. Made last night.
concrete_content: Seven or eight of them. They crossed here after dark heading toward the old ruins on the ridge. They did not camp — they kept moving, which means they have a destination.
tension: They passed through. They are ahead now, not behind. The road is clear but the ridge is not.
traveller_stance: Reads the tracks. Adjusts the route to keep the ridge on the left.

npc_attitude: Five of them blocking the road. The one in front is doing the talking. The others are watching the flanks.
concrete_content: They want to know what the traveller is carrying. They are not asking to decide whether to rob them — they have already decided. They are asking to decide how much violence the robbery requires.
tension: The question buys thirty seconds. The answer determines whether those seconds cost anything.
traveller_stance: Does not answer the question. Reads the spacing. Decides which one to move toward.

npc_attitude: Three of them at a chokepoint — a defile between rocks, unavoidable. They saw the traveller before the traveller saw them.
concrete_content: The captain — bigger, scarred across one eye — has a specific instruction: no killing on the road, bring back anyone found alive. This is unusual. Someone above him wants information, not corpses.
tension: The restraint is not mercy. It is orders. The orders may be more dangerous than simple violence.
traveller_stance: Hears the difference between kill-on-sight and bring-back. Adjusts accordingly.

npc_attitude: A full patrol — eight, plus a warg on a chain held by the largest. They have been tracking for a while.
concrete_content: The warg has a scent. It is pulling toward the traveller. The captain is watching the warg and watching the traveller and making the same calculation simultaneously.
tension: The warg knows. The captain knows the warg knows. The moment the captain decides to trust the warg is the moment the conversation ends.
traveller_stance: Has approximately ten seconds before the captain decides.

npc_attitude: A smear of dark grease on a rock at the trail's edge — orc scout-mark. They passed here within six hours.
concrete_content: The mark faces back the way the traveller came, not forward. They are behind the traveller on this road, moving in the same direction.
tension: They are scouts, not raiders. They are following to report, not to engage. Not yet.
traveller_stance: Does not change pace. Changes route at the next junction.

npc_attitude: Two of them in the rocks above the road. One is watching the traveller. The other is looking back the way the traveller came.
concrete_content: They have not moved since the traveller came into view. They are deciding whether to signal or stay quiet. The one watching the road behind keeps checking — they are waiting for something larger to follow the traveller.
tension: They are a forward element. What they are waiting for is behind the traveller on this road.
traveller_stance: Does not look up. Does not slow. Finds cover before the junction.

npc_attitude: One of them in a hollow, motionless. Visible only because of a mistake in wind direction.
concrete_content: Alone scout, likely separated from a larger group. Watching a specific point on the road ahead — a junction, or an approach to something he is meant to mark.
tension: He has not seen Aranath. If he reports back, the road ahead is compromised. If he does not report back, the group he serves knows within hours.
traveller_stance: Has three options. Runs the cost of each. Chooses the one that costs the road least.

npc_attitude: One of them in a hollow, motionless. She saw him before he settled — the way he moved to cover was practiced but not perfect.
concrete_content: She has seen orc scouts for longer than this particular breed has existed. This one is young by the signs — the crouch is right but he keeps shifting weight. He is watching the road junction ahead, not her approach.
tension: If she passes, he will see her and report. The question is whether she is willing to be reported.
traveller_stance: Walks the road at the exact pace that makes her uninteresting. Does not look at the hollow.

npc_attitude: Three of them, moving fast on a scent. They run into the traveller coming the other way on the road.
concrete_content: They are on the trail of someone specific — not the traveller. For ten seconds they are unsure whether to abandon the trail and deal with the obstacle or push past. The captain is deciding.
tension: The scent they are following went through here recently. If the traveller knows where it leads, they are suddenly useful alive.
traveller_stance: Lets them decide. Does not volunteer information about the trail.

npc_attitude: The tracks on the road are layered — someone passed here running, and then, later, something followed them.
concrete_content: The first tracks are a day old. The second set are hours old and moving fast. The second set are orc-boots, wide-spaced. Pursuit gait.
tension: Someone on this road is being hunted. They are ahead of the trackers. Both of them are ahead of the traveller.
traveller_stance: Reads the timing. Continues on the road knowing what is ahead of them.

npc_attitude: A column on the road below — forty, fifty, moving south with supplies. Torches though it is still light. They are not hiding.
concrete_content: They have a specific destination and they know the road is theirs right now. The banner they carry is one that has not been seen on this road in decades.
tension: A warband this size moving openly means someone has decided the road belongs to the Enemy again. That decision was made recently.
traveller_stance: Does not go down to the road. Watches until the column passes. Then waits another hour.

npc_attitude: A warband crossing a ridge ahead — visible for sixty seconds before they drop into the valley. Forty at least.
concrete_content: Their direction takes them across the Ranger patrol circuit at a point he knows. If they reach the circuit before he does, three villages lose their warning system.
tension: He cannot stop them. He can get ahead of them.
traveller_stance: Counts them. Identifies the banner. Moves.

npc_attitude: A warband crossing a ridge ahead. She has seen larger. She has seen what they leave.
concrete_content: The banner. She knows that banner. She has seen it before, in a different Age, on a different ridge. It should not exist anymore — the line that carried it was broken in the Second Age.
tension: Something reconstituted that banner. Something that remembers the Second Age well enough to reconstruct its symbols.
traveller_stance: Files the banner. Continues. This is not a coincidence.

npc_attitude: A supply caravan on the road below — slow, heavily loaded, ill-tempered guards. Some of the figures at the center are not orcs.
concrete_content: The non-orc figures move differently from the guards — shorter steps, heads down. They are captives being moved, not cargo. Three of them.
tension: The guards are watching the road, not the captives. The captives are watching the ground.
traveller_stance: Does not go down to the road.

npc_attitude: A caravan from above — slow, supply-laden, three captives at the center under light guard.
concrete_content: The guards are four, plus a drover at the front who is not paying attention to anything but the pace. The captives are bound at the wrist but their legs are free.
tension: He has run the calculation three times and the numbers are the same each time. Four guards, three captives, open road. The question is not whether he can do it — it is whether he can do it without the captives dying in the confusion.
traveller_stance: Finds the angle. Finds the timing. Decides.

npc_attitude: A caravan below. Three captives. She has seen this before more times than she can count.
concrete_content: The captives are alive and moving under their own power. Alive and moving means there is still time. She has never once convinced herself that was enough reason not to act.
tension: She is not going to walk past.
traveller_stance: Goes down to the road.

npc_attitude: A column of driven captives — mixed, twenty of them — moving under orc-whip along a side-track below the road.
concrete_content: One of the captives at the edge of the column catches sight of the traveller above. Does not call out. Holds the gaze for three seconds, then looks back at the ground.
tension: The captive saw them. The guards did not. The captive made a choice not to endanger the traveller. That choice cost something.
traveller_stance: Holds the gaze for the three seconds. Then continues.

npc_attitude: The column has stopped — a broken wheel on the supply cart, guards occupied. One captive at the edge, close enough to the treeline to speak quietly.
concrete_content: He was a farmer from a settlement three days north. They were taken eleven days ago. He does not know where they are going. He asks for nothing except that someone knows they are here, that someone saw them.
tension: He is not asking to be saved right now. He is asking to be witnessed.
traveller_stance: Says: I see you. I will remember this road.

npc_attitude: A traveller on the road — unremarkable, moving south, nods in passing.
concrete_content: Something is slightly wrong about the face. The proportions are almost right. The nod was timed correctly but not reflexively.
tension: They are passing as human. They are nearly doing it successfully.
traveller_stance: Notes what is wrong about the face. Does not indicate it. Continues.

npc_attitude: A trader at a wayhouse — dark-complected, broad, asking questions about the road north in a way that sounds casual but is building a picture.
concrete_content: The questions are too specific to be traveller-curiosity. He wants to know where the patrols are, not where the road conditions are. He is gathering operational intelligence dressed as conversation.
tension: He does not know yet that the person he is asking knows what he is.
traveller_stance: Answers the questions with plausible misdirection. Files the encounter.

npc_attitude: A mercenary at the same wayhouse fire. Has been there when he arrived, will still be there when he leaves.
concrete_content: The jaw is wrong. The hands are wrong — the knuckles sit differently, the reach is too long for the body. He has been watching everyone who enters and noting who they speak to.
tension: Aranath has seen half-orcs before. This one does not yet know he has been identified.
traveller_stance: Takes the seat across the fire. Speaks first. Watches what the mercenary does with the initiative.

npc_attitude: A figure on the road ahead, waiting. Standing in the middle, not to the side. He has been waiting for her specifically.
concrete_content: He has a message. He delivers it without ceremony: she is known on this road, she has been seen, and there are those who would prefer she did not continue south.
tension: The message was crafted by someone intelligent. The messenger was chosen because he could deliver it without being dismissed as an animal.
traveller_stance: Asks who sends it. The answer is not one she expected.

npc_attitude: Three of them on the road in full daylight, not bothering to hide. That alone is the warning.
concrete_content: The captain speaks in rough Westron. He wants the traveller to stop, turn around, and not use this road again. He says this once, clearly, without threat in his voice. The threat is in what he is.
tension: The message is orderly. The instruction is reasonable enough to follow. That is what makes it worse than a simple ambush.
traveller_stance: Stops. Asks on whose authority.

npc_attitude: Five of them at a bridge. They have been there for a day at least — the camp-sign is established. They are not raiders. They are a garrison.
concrete_content: The bridge is the only crossing for miles. They are not here by accident and they are not here temporarily. Someone placed them here to control this crossing.
tension: This is not a patrol. This is occupation. The difference matters for what comes next.
traveller_stance: Does not approach the bridge. Looks for the ford.

npc_attitude: Five of them at the bridge. The captain looks at him with the specific attention of someone who has been given a description to look for.
concrete_content: The description partially matches. The captain knows this. He is going to confirm before acting, because he is disciplined enough to not kill the wrong person.
tension: The confirmation question will come. The answer determines whether this ends as a conversation or something else.
traveller_stance: Answers the first question before it is asked. Watches the captain decide whether the answer fits.

npc_attitude: Warg-prints crossing the road at speed — four sets, all heading east. The prints are deep and fresh.
concrete_content: They were moving at a run. Four wargs running east on this road within the last two hours means a patrol, a pursuit, or a message being carried fast.
tension: Whatever they were doing, they are east of here now and moving.
traveller_stance: Notes the direction. Continues west.

npc_attitude: A sound before the sight — hooves but wrong, too heavy, wrong cadence. Then three of them cresting a rise ahead at speed.
concrete_content: They have not seen the traveller yet. They will in approximately eight seconds. They are moving too fast to stop cleanly.
tension: Eight seconds is enough if used correctly.
traveller_stance: Uses them correctly.

npc_attitude: A chamber off the main passage — three of them around a flat stone. Ritual, not preparation for violence.
concrete_content: They are speaking in a dialect she has not heard in centuries — not Black Speech exactly, something older, something that predates the standardization of the Enemy's tongue.
tension: They have not heard the traveller. The dialect they are using is a version of something she knows.
traveller_stance: Does not enter the chamber. Does not reveal presence. Listens.

npc_attitude: One of them in a passage, alone, carrying something wrapped. He sees her before she sees him. He does not run.
concrete_content: He speaks — not in threat, in shock. He says a name in a tongue she has not heard used by orcs before. The name is for something she knows. He has no right to know it.
tension: He knows that name. That name should be known only by those who were there when it was given. She was there. He was not.
traveller_stance: Asks him where he heard that name. The answer is worse than any she would have constructed herself.

npc_attitude: A passage that seemed clear until it was not. Three of them blocking the way back, not forward — they came from behind.
concrete_content: They are not fighting. They are herding. They want the intruder away from a specific section of the passage — whatever is there, they are protecting it.
tension: They are not going to let the traveller go back toward whatever they are guarding. Forward is the only option they are offering.
traveller_stance: Goes forward. Notes where they would not let them go back.

npc_attitude: A checkpoint — two orcs and one Man, at a gap in a collapsed wall that serves as the only pass through a ridge.
concrete_content: The Man does the speaking. He asks for papers. Not a robbery — a genuine administrative checkpoint. Whatever Angmar is doing in this territory, it has bureaucracy.
tension: Papers exist for people who are supposed to be here. The traveller is not supposed to be here.
traveller_stance: Has no papers. Has a story instead. Watches to see which one the Man prefers.

npc_attitude: A patrol that knows exactly what a Ranger looks like. The orc-captain speaks better Westron than expected.
concrete_content: He says that Rangers of the North are not welcome in the Witch-king's lands and that this one has two choices: leave the road immediately or be escorted to the nearest tower, from which he will not leave.
tension: The Witch-king's lands extend further south than they did a year ago. Aranath is standing in them.
traveller_stance: Measures the distance to the road's edge. Then measures the distance to the tower he can see behind the patrol. Leaves the road.

npc_attitude: Three of them at the edge of the ice, in daylight — they do not flinch from it. Watching the traveller on the snowfield below.
concrete_content: They endure sunlight. This alone is the warning — whatever adjustments made common orcs into these, the result is something that does not have the usual weakness.
tension: They are deciding whether the traveller is worth the effort of the snowfield between them.
traveller_stance: Does not run. Running on a snowfield goes badly. Continues at the same pace.

npc_attitude: Sound before sight — many footsteps in the dark, coordinated. Then torchlight. Too much torchlight for a patrol.
concrete_content: A hunting-party, not a patrol. They were not looking for the traveller specifically — they were sweeping the passage. The traveller was in the passage.
tension: The sweep is methodical. They have done this before. This passage has one exit behind and one exit forward.
traveller_stance: Moves toward the forward exit. Does not run yet.

npc_attitude: A single old orc at a junction — not patrol, not hunting. Sitting.
concrete_content: He has been in these halls since before Durin's Bane came. He says this without boasting — a statement of fact from someone too old to care about impressions. He watches her with the specific recognition of someone who knew this place when it was something else.
tension: He knew Khazad-dum when Dwarves still lived in it. She also knew Khazad-dum when Dwarves still lived in it. They are the only two in this passage who share that.
traveller_stance: Sits across the junction. Asks him what he remembers of the forges on the third level.

npc_attitude: A court — rough, filthy, loud — and at the center of it a thing that is very large and very old and speaks with the ease of someone who has not needed to hurry in a long time.
concrete_content: He already knows who the traveller is. He says this first. He has been told. He does not say by whom. He says there is a negotiation to be had before he decides what to do with them.
tension: He is old enough to prefer manipulation to violence when manipulation is available. That is more dangerous, not less.
traveller_stance: Listens to what the negotiation is. Does not agree to anything immediately.

npc_attitude: Three of them around a fire on the road, arguing about whether last week's shepherd was better roasted or boiled. They have not noticed the traveller yet.
concrete_content: The argument is genuine — they have strong opinions about cooking methods and the debate has been going since before the fire was lit. They are not watchful. They are occupied.
tension: Three Stone-trolls arguing about food are, for the moment, not paying attention to anything else. Dawn is four hours away.
traveller_stance: Does not step on anything. Does not breathe loudly. Moves around the fire's light.

npc_attitude: One of them blocking the road. The other two are somewhere behind him — audible but not visible.
concrete_content: He wants to know if the traveller has seen any sheep in the last valley. He genuinely wants to know. He has lost his flock.
tension: He is asking a question and waiting for an answer. He is not going to move until he gets one. He is also blocking a road at night, which is its own problem regardless of his intentions.
traveller_stance: Answers the question. Tells him the direction. Watches him go.

npc_attitude: Three of them at the crossroads, and they have seen him. The largest one speaks first.
concrete_content: He says — slow, deliberate — that he remembers a man who looked like this man coming through here three years ago and that man had a good knife. He wants to know if this man has a good knife.
tension: He is not asking to rob it. He genuinely wants a good knife. The previous man gave it to him voluntarily. The memory is oddly specific.
traveller_stance: Thinks about what he can spare. Considers what walking out of the Trollshaws is worth.

npc_attitude: Three of them at a fire. One looks up when she passes — she is apparently the most interesting thing that has happened since the shepherd argument.
concrete_content: He asks what she is. Not threatening — he genuinely does not know. He has seen Elves before but they were smaller and they ran away. She is not running away.
tension: The question is curious, not hostile. Curiosity is a window.
traveller_stance: Tells him what she is. Asks what he is. He finds this extremely funny.

npc_attitude: One on the road — not a full ambush, just one, and it is looking at the traveller with the specific dull focus of a creature that has decided something is going to happen.
concrete_content: It does not speak first. It is waiting to see what the traveller does. This is how it hunts — it lets the prey decide whether to run, because running is interesting.
tension: Running is interesting. Standing is boring. Boring might be survivable.
traveller_stance: Does not run. Does not retreat. Stands.

npc_attitude: Two of them, and they have been waiting — the approach was too convenient, the gap in the rocks too obvious. This is practiced.
concrete_content: The first one speaks — three words, badly articulated, but in Westron: give it here. It does not specify what it. Everything the traveller is carrying qualifies.
tension: The other one is circling. The circling is the real danger.
traveller_stance: Does not look at the circling one. Keeps eyes on the one that spoke.

npc_attitude: It moved — something that had been a fallen trunk is not a fallen trunk anymore. It is standing up.
concrete_content: It has been waiting in place long enough that moss has grown on its back. This is a troll that understands patience. It is between the traveller and the path forward.
tension: It did not try to hide, exactly. It simply did not move. The forest made it invisible by being itself around it.
traveller_stance: Has approximately four options. Three of them are bad.

npc_attitude: One on the road in full daylight. Armored. Not a foraging beast — a soldier.
concrete_content: It does not speak. It is not here to speak. It is here because it was placed here and it will remain here until it is removed or its purpose is accomplished.
tension: This is a weapon pointed at a road. The traveller is on the road.
traveller_stance: Looks for what is behind it — what it is guarding. Then looks for the way around.

npc_attitude: A soldier at a garrison post — large, dark, speaks Westron with a southerner's accent. The garrison is Harad-aligned.
concrete_content: He asks for road-papers in the manner of someone following procedure. The procedure is real. He is following it correctly. He is also seven feet tall and the wrong color for what he is claiming to be.
tension: The procedure is a distraction. The question is what happens when the procedure is satisfied.
traveller_stance: Produces what papers exist. Watches how he reads them.

npc_attitude: Three of them — small for trolls, which still means large — circling something dead on the road ahead. They have not noticed the traveller yet.
concrete_content: They are arguing over the dead thing. One of them wants it, the other two want it, and none of them have worked out a resolution. The argument is escalating toward something physical.
tension: Three young trolls about to start fighting over food is a window. The road goes around them on the right.
traveller_stance: Takes the right side. Moves fast.

npc_attitude: A figure at the far end of a ruined hall — standing still, looking at the traveller. It has no shadow.
concrete_content: It does not move. It is waiting to be acknowledged. Ghosts of this kind — the old ones, the ones who remember themselves clearly — need acknowledgment before they can speak.
tension: It has been here a long time. Whatever it is waiting to say has not diminished in the waiting.
traveller_stance: Acknowledges it. Waits.

npc_attitude: A man in old Arnorian gear — the cut is two centuries out of fashion — at the edge of a barrow. He speaks when Aranath approaches.
concrete_content: He says he was the last warden of a specific tower on the Weather Hills. He wants to know if the tower is still standing. He asks it simply, like a man asking after a house he built.
tension: The tower has been rubble for a hundred and forty years. He does not know this.
traveller_stance: Tells him the tower fell. Watches the ghost receive this.

npc_attitude: A woman — Dúnedain, by the gear, Second Age by the style — standing at a crossroads and looking at the two roads with the expression of someone who cannot remember which way she was going.
concrete_content: She asks Celebrian which road leads east. She says her husband is east, waiting. She says this with the certainty of someone who has been saying it for a very long time.
tension: She died before Celebrian came to this road. She has been at this crossroads since. The husband she is waiting for is dust.
traveller_stance: Tells her which road leads east. Does not tell her the rest.

npc_attitude: It appears as someone she knew — the resemblance is close enough that she stops.
concrete_content: It is not him. The face is right but the eyes are wrong — they are looking at her but seeing something she cannot see. It speaks in a voice that is almost his and says her name in a way that is almost how he said it.
tension: It is a ghost that has learned to wear a shape it found in her memory. Ghosts do not do this spontaneously.
traveller_stance: Does not engage with the shape. Asks what it actually is.

npc_attitude: A ghost at a barrow-mound — Dunedain, old, carrying wounds that never healed. It speaks before he can.
concrete_content: It says: you are one of us. It says: you will be here too, soon enough, if you keep walking this road. It says it without malice — a statement of fact from someone standing on the other side of it.
tension: The ghost is not threatening him. It is warning him. The distinction is the most unsettling part.
traveller_stance: Asks it what is on this road.

npc_attitude: The road empties ahead. Sound drops out. The cold comes first, then the shape on the road — and the shape has been there the whole time, it was always there, the traveller simply did not see it until now.
concrete_content: It does not speak first. It waits. The waiting is the weapon — every second of it drains something.
tension: Whatever the Witch-king wants from this road today, the traveller is now in the calculation.
traveller_stance: Does not move. Does not flee. Waits for it to speak.

npc_attitude: The shape on the road. The cold. The specific silence that precedes it.
concrete_content: It speaks. It says his name. Not his road-name — his true name, the one only the Dunedain records carry. It says: I have read those records.
tension: It knows who he is. It has taken the time to know who he is. That is not a good sign.
traveller_stance: Answers with his name said back at it. Watches whether that matters.

npc_attitude: The shape on the road. The Noldor feel the cold differently — not fear exactly, but the specific recognition of something that has been enemy longer than most things have existed.
concrete_content: It speaks. In a language she has not heard spoken aloud since the Second Age — the court tongue of Númenor in its darkest years. It says, in that language: you should have sailed.
tension: It knows what she is and what she chose and it is using that choice against her.
traveller_stance: Answers it in the same tongue. Says: so should you.

npc_attitude: Something white and cold at the passage's end — not a light, not a figure exactly, but something that is organizing itself into a shape.
concrete_content: It has not attacked. Specters do not attack — they approach, and the approaching is the attack. It is between the traveller and the forward exit.
tension: Moving toward it makes it worse. Moving away from it is also moving away from the exit.
traveller_stance: Does not move toward it. Finds the secondary exit that all Khazad passages have.

npc_attitude: A man in brown — very brown, and some of it is not dye — crouching beside the road examining something in the grass. He does not look up when the traveller approaches.
concrete_content: He says, without looking up, that there is a nest of harriers three miles north that has been disturbed — not by a fox, by something heavier, moving on two feet, three nights running. Then he looks up and seems mildly surprised there is a person here.
tension: The animal intelligence is specific and accurate. The information about what disturbed the nest is more useful than he realizes he has given.
traveller_stance: Asks what two-footed thing moves at night for three nights in the same area.

npc_attitude: He appears out of the forest at the road's edge with two ravens on his shoulders who are arguing with each other about something he keeps trying to mediate.
concrete_content: The ravens have been following something south from the Carrock for a week. They are arguing about what it is. He translates both positions with the patience of a man who has been doing this for a very long time. The disagreement is: one says orc-warband, the other says it is organized differently.
tension: He is giving them this information because he believes travelers should know what the ravens know. He does not consider whether the traveler wants it.
traveller_stance: Asks which direction it is organized toward.

npc_attitude: He steps onto the road and stops Aranath specifically — he has been waiting.
concrete_content: He says that the animals on the north road have gone quiet for two miles in a specific direction, and that this kind of quiet has happened twice before that he remembers, and both times it preceded something the Rangers needed to know about.
tension: He is not sure what it means. He is sure enough to stop a Ranger and say it.
traveller_stance: Asks where the quiet starts.

npc_attitude: He recognizes her immediately — or rather, one of the ravens on his shoulder does, and tells him, and he reacts to what the raven says rather than to her directly.
concrete_content: He says the raven says she smells like someone who has been in Aman and chose to leave it. The raven is apparently very specific about this. He apologizes for the raven.
tension: The raven is right. The apology is genuine. He is faintly embarrassed by his own ability.
traveller_stance: Tells the raven it is correct. Watches Radagast receive this secondhand.

npc_attitude: He comes to her directly, without the usual roundabout. He looks at her with the specific attention of a Maia who has seen something he did not expect to see.
concrete_content: He says that the forest between here and the mountains is wrong in a way it has not been wrong since the Second Age. He says it in the voice of someone who has been trying to dismiss this observation for a week and can no longer.
tension: He wants to know if she has felt it too. He is asking because if she has not felt it, he can continue to dismiss it. If she has, he cannot.
traveller_stance: Tells him what she has felt. Watches him decide what to do with the confirmation.

npc_attitude: He is already there when the traveller arrives at the river crossing — sitting on a log singing something about kingfishers that is not quite a song and not quite a spell and is definitely working on something in the water.
concrete_content: He looks up and says: there you are! As if he was expecting them. He may have been. He invites them to dinner without asking their name, which he already knows.
tension: He is the most helpful and least explicable thing on this road. The help is genuine. The inexplicability is permanent.
traveller_stance: Accepts the dinner. Does not ask how he knew.

npc_attitude: He appears on the path ahead, walking in the opposite direction, and greets the traveller as though they are old friends who have not seen each other in a week.
concrete_content: He has been watching something in the Old Forest that has been behaving unusually for the past month — Old Man Willow, he says, has been dreaming worse than usual, and the dreams are coming from outside Old Man Willow, not from inside. He says this the way someone reports a neighbor's cough.
tension: The information is more significant than his tone suggests. He knows this. He is telling the traveller because travelers carry information forward.
traveller_stance: Asks what outside means.

npc_attitude: He finds her on the road and simply walks beside her for a while. He does not speak for several minutes, which for him is unusual.
concrete_content: Then he says: something is asking questions about you. Not looking for you — asking questions about you. There is a difference, and the difference is that asking questions means someone is deciding whether to look.
tension: He is not afraid for her. He is telling her because she should know. He will not tell her who is asking because he does not know.
traveller_stance: Asks when the questions started.

npc_attitude: Nothing on the road. No encounter. A feeling only — that something at a great distance has become aware of the traveller's presence on this specific road.
concrete_content: The feeling is not imagination. It is the particular sensation of being indexed — catalogued, noted, filed. Not targeted yet. Just noted.
tension: Something in Dol Guldur knows the traveller is on this road. It has not decided yet what to do about it.
traveller_stance: Continues. Does not look toward the south-east.

npc_attitude: A pull — not physical, not voice. A direction. Something far south-east that is aware of her and wants her to be aware of it in return.
concrete_content: She has felt this before. The last time she felt it, Sauron was not yet in hiding. It is subtler now — the same pressure compressed into a point, wearing a disguise she can almost see through.
tension: She does not respond to it. Responding confirms the contact. She has been doing this long enough to know that.
traveller_stance: Does not look south-east. Does not acknowledge. Continues.

npc_attitude: A tree that is watching — not moving, not speaking, but watching. The watching is distinct from the passive quality of the forest.
concrete_content: It has been aware of the traveller since they entered the forest. It has not decided what to do about this yet. Ents are slow to decide.
tension: The decision, when it comes, will be final. In the meantime, the forest simply watches.
traveller_stance: Moves without haste. Does not break branches. Does not take anything.

npc_attitude: A voice — slow, deep, coming from above, and it takes a moment to understand that the tree it is coming from has eyes.
concrete_content: It asks what the traveller wants in the forest. The question takes approximately forty-five seconds to ask. It is waiting for a real answer, not a quick one.
tension: The Ent has been asking this question for a long time. It has learned to tell the difference between an answer and a performance of an answer.
traveller_stance: Takes the time the question deserves. Answers honestly.

npc_attitude: It knows what she is. It greets her with a name for her kind that has not been used since the First Age.
concrete_content: It says, slowly, that the forest remembers when the Noldor came east from Aman, and the forest has been watching what happened since. It says this without accusation — the way you note a very long consequence.
tension: She is part of a very long consequence. She knows this. It is something else entirely to have a tree tell her so.
traveller_stance: Sits down at its roots. Asks what the forest saw.

npc_attitude: Something enormous has turned to look at him. This takes a while.
concrete_content: It asks, eventually, whether he is one of the tree-shepherds' kin — meaning the Dunedain rangers who have been keeping Fangorn's eastern edge for generations. The question is asked with recognition, not suspicion.
tension: It knows about the Rangers. Not this Ranger specifically — the Rangers as a category of thing it has observed.
traveller_stance: Confirms it. Asks what the Ents have seen on the eastern edge recently.

npc_attitude: A small party on the road, laden and purposeful. One of them looks at her for a long moment before the others notice.
concrete_content: They are heading to the markets of Men with metalwork. The one who looked has seen Elves before — the look is not awe, it is recognition of age. He asks, without prelude, whether she was in the mountains when the first shafts were sunk.
tension: He is asking because his clan's founding records reference an Elf who witnessed the first stone-cut. He wants to know if she is that Elf.
traveller_stance: Considers the timeframe. Tells him she was not there at the beginning but knew those who were.

npc_attitude: Blunt assessment, then dealing. He does not waste time on pleasantries.
concrete_content: He has a map of a vein in the Blue Mountains that predates the current Dwarf-holds. The landmarks are named in a tongue he does not recognize. He wants someone who might.
tension: The tongue on the map is Quenya. She recognizes three of the four landmarks. The fourth no longer exists.
traveller_stance: Reads the map. Names what she can. Tells him what the missing landmark was.

npc_attitude: Watchful. Something on this road has made the group cautious and they extend that caution to her.
concrete_content: They passed through a mountain pass two days ago and something in it was wrong — not orcs, not trolls. A sound in the deep stone that none of them had heard before. They mention it because she looks old enough to have a name for it.
tension: She has a name for it. The name is not reassuring.
traveller_stance: Tells them what it is. Watches them decide what to do with that information.

npc_attitude: Footsore, carrying too much, heading somewhere with the specific determination of someone who has not thought through what they will do when they arrive.
concrete_content: He is looking for a relative who went east to trade and stopped writing letters. The relative has been gone eight months. The hobbit has been walking for three weeks and is running out of food.
tension: He is absurdly ill-equipped for this road and he knows it and he is going anyway. The stubbornness is oddly affecting.
traveller_stance: Gives him what food she can spare. Tells him which road is safer.

npc_attitude: He has been on this road too long. Something in his face has changed from the way hobbits usually look.
concrete_content: He found his relative — or found where the relative had been. He is not going to say what he found. He is heading home now. He says this very quietly.
tension: He walked into something he was not made for and came out the other side. He is still coming out the other side.
traveller_stance: Does not ask what he found. Walks with him for half a mile. Then lets him go.

npc_attitude: Three of them on the road in full daylight. The one in front is watching her the way a professional watches a problem.
concrete_content: He speaks in rough Westron: this road is closed. He does not say by whose authority. He does not need to — the daylight is the authority. Common orcs do not walk roads in daylight.
tension: He is following orders, not impulse. She is an obstacle in his orders. The question is whether she is an obstacle worth the effort.
traveller_stance: Asks whose road this is. Waits to see how he answers.

npc_attitude: Five of them holding a bridge. They have been there long enough to establish a camp. This is occupation, not patrol.
concrete_content: The captain has a specific description he is checking travellers against. He studies her for a long moment. She matches something in his description closely enough that he is not going to let her cross without questions.
tension: She has been looked at this way before by things that served darker masters than this one. The calibration runs automatically.
traveller_stance: Answers the first question before he asks it. Watches whether that unsettles him.

npc_attitude: A mixed patrol — orcs and Men together under Angmar's banner. The Man does the speaking.
concrete_content: He asks for papers in Westron. Behind him, one of the orcs is watching her with the specific attention of something that recognizes what she is. The Man does not notice the orc's attention.
tension: The Man is doing a job. The orc behind him knows what she is and is deciding whether to say so to the Man.
traveller_stance: Keeps her attention on the Man. Does not look at the orc.

npc_attitude: The patrol has stopped her before she reached the checkpoint — they were watching the approach.
concrete_content: The captain knows who she is. Not her name — what she is. He has been told to watch for an elf travelling alone and he has been very thorough about what that means. He is also afraid, in the way men in Angmar's service learn to be afraid.
tension: He is afraid of what will happen if he lets her pass and more afraid of what will happen if he tries to stop her and fails.
traveller_stance: Lets the fear be visible to him. Then walks past.

npc_attitude: Alone on the road with a full pack, moving at the steady pace of someone who has been walking for days and will walk for days more.
concrete_content: He nods at Aranath in the way travelers acknowledge each other on empty roads. Then, after a few steps, stops. Asks which direction the nearest garrison is. Not the road — the garrison specifically.
tension: He wants to know where armed men are, not merchants. Something on the road behind him.
traveller_stance: Tells him the garrison direction. Asks what is on the road behind him.

npc_attitude: At a wayhouse fire, eating alone, not talking to anyone.
